sections.header: Verses 1‐3 — Jehu’s Rebuke for the Ahab Alliance

panels.ctx: Jehu son of Hanani meets Jehoshaphat: “Should you help the wicked and love those who hate the LORD? Because of this, the wrath of the LORD is on you.” But he adds: “There is, however, some good in you, for you have rid the land of the Asherah poles and have set your heart on seeking God.” Partial judgment for partial failure — the Chronicler’s proportional retribution.

notes.note: The principle demonstrated here applies universally: God’s governance is consistent, and human choices produce proportional divine responses.

net.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: The Chronicler’s characteristic vocabulary and theological framework shape the presentation of these events.

sections.header: Verses 4‐11 — Judges Appointed; “Judge Carefully, for the LORD Is with You”

panels.ctx: Jehoshaphat appoints judges throughout Judah with a charge unique to Chronicles: “Consider carefully what you do, because you are not judging for mere mortals but for the LORD, who is with you whenever you give a verdict. Judge carefully, for with the LORD our God there is no injustice or partiality or bribery.” He establishes a central court in Jerusalem with Levites, priests, and clan leaders. Justice is both local and centralised, both divine and institutional.

notes.note: The principle demonstrated here applies universally: God’s governance is consistent, and human choices produce proportional divine responses.

net.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: The Chronicler’s characteristic vocabulary and theological framework shape the presentation of these events.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVHe told them, \u201cConsider carefully what you do, because you are not judging for mere mortals but for the Lord, who is with you whenever you give a verdict.ESVand said to the judges, \u201cConsider what you do, for you judge not for man but for the LORD. He is with you in giving judgment.NIVJehu the seer, the son of Hanani, went out to meet him and said to the king, \u201cShould you help the wicked and love those who hate the Lord? Because of this, the wrath of the Lord is on you.ESVBut Jehu the son of Hanani the seer went out to meet him and said to King Jehoshaphat, \u201cShould you help the wicked and love those who hate the LORD? Because of this, wrath has gone out against you from the LORD.
